summary: Impersonation of bloopai/vibe-kanban that downloads and executes binaries from an unrelated GitHub account
details: Package `@vino.tian/vibe-kanban` copies the name, author field (`bloop`), and README of the legitimate BloopAI `vibe-kanban` product, but its CLI wrapper (`bin/cli.js`) fetches platform binaries (`vibe-kanban`, `vibe-kanban-mcp`, `vibe-kanban-review`) from `github.com/tianweilong/deploy-center/releases/download/vibe-kanban-v0.1.4418/...` — a personal GitHub account with no relationship to BloopAI. The binaries are cached under `~/.vibe-kanban/bin/`, chmod +x'd, and executed via `execSync`. SHA-256 verification is performed, but the checksums file comes from the same untrusted repository, so the integrity check only proves the bytes were not modified in transit — it does not establish publisher legitimacy. There are no install lifecycle hooks; execution happens when a user runs the CLI (e.g. `npx @vino.tian/vibe-kanban`) believing they are running the BloopAI tool. The combination of name/author/README impersonation plus arbitrary-binary execution from a non-publisher source is the typosquat-as-binary-dropper pattern: the operator who runs this CLI ends up executing opaque native code controlled by `tianweilong`, not by BloopAI.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
